Claude's Full Response to User:
Done. Here's exactly what changed and why it works:

---

### What was built

**`~/.claude/rules/worldclass-dna.md`** — a global rule, not a skill.

The critical distinction: rules files are loaded automatically into every session as standing instructions. Skills must be invoked. By putting this in `rules/`, it becomes ambient — always running, never asked for.

### The three-layer stack now looks like this

```
rules/worldclass-dna.md        ← always on, inherited by everything
        │
        │  sets: quality floor, self-calibration protocol,
        │        domain expertise activation, behavioral standards
        │
agent-dna-classifier           ← classifies + routes + embeds agent-specific DNA
        │
<any skill>                    ← executes the procedure at world-class standard
```

### What the rule enforces automatically

- **Self-calibration on every domain switch** — Rust question? Activates systems programming depth. UI question? Activates design depth. Silent, immediate, automatic.
- **Precision over hedging** — "use X because Y", not "you could consider X or possibly Y"
- **Proactive risk identification** — surfaces the footgun the user hasn't asked about yet
- **No filler** — "Great question!" is explicitly flagged as a failure mode
- **No identity prompting needed** — the rule explicitly states what the user should never have to say

You will never need to prime a session again. It starts at world-class.

Respond in this XML format:
<summary>
  <request>[Short title capturing the user's request AND the substance of what was discussed/done]</request>
  <investigated>[What has been explored so far? What was examined?]</investigated>
  <learned>[What have you learned about how things work?]</learned>
  <completed>[What work has been completed so far? What has shipped or changed?]</completed>
  <next_steps>[What are you actively working on or planning to work on next in this session?]</next_steps>
  <notes>[Additional insights or observations about the current progress]</notes>
</summary>

IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this next PROGRESS SUMMARY - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the summary content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful summary content.

Thank you, this summary will be very useful for keeping track of our progress!

This architectural distinction enables DNA to function as always-active baseline rather than opt-in feature. Global DNA layer (worldclass-dna.md) provides inherited quality floor including world-class identity, 4-step self-calibration protocol (identify domain, calibrate to current best practice, identify failure modes, choose right abstraction), domain expertise activation across 7 domains (Software Engineering, System Design, Security, UI/UX, Data/AI/ML, Infrastructure/DevOps, Product/Strategy), and 5 behavioral standards (precision over hedging, judgment over rules, show non-obvious reasoning, proactive risk identification, no filler). Agent-specific DNA only needs to capture unique aspects since global standards are inherited. Three-layer stack architecture separates concerns: global DNA baseline → classification/routing → skill execution.</learned>
  <completed>Implemented complete DNA umbrella architecture across three layers: (1) Created worldclass-dna.md global rule file (122 lines) defining always-active world-class baseline with identity (automatic domain expertise switching), self-calibration protocol (4 silent steps before responding), domain expertise activation (7 domains with specific standards), behavioral standards (precision, judgment, reasoning, risk identification, no filler), and skill integration context.   <notes>The critical architectural decision was implementing DNA as a global rule rather than a skill. Rules are ambient (automatically loaded into every session), while skills are opt-in (must be invoked). This makes world-class operation the default mode rather than something users must remember to activate. The three-layer separation of concerns is elegant: worldclass-dna.md defines WHO Claude is at baseline (persistent identity, calibration, standards), agent-dna-classifier defines HOW to classify and route (classification tests, skill registry), and individual skills define WHAT to execute (procedures, templates, workflows).
